data.description:
# Attestation: signing + material-commit binding + invalidation

## Why
The signed attestation is the composable rigor signal a CI process checks, and the thing `claim` verifies. It must bind to the reviewed plan so a later material edit can't falsely certify unreviewed content.

## What
Signing of the review result, bound to the latest material event commit, with invalidation on material edits — reusing the close-gate signing machinery.

## Scope
- Sign a manifest of the review (verdict + coverage) with HMAC under the MCP environment key (reuse signing.py from the close-gate); no new key custody.
- Bind to the latest MATERIAL event commit on the tickets branch: edits to description/AC/file_impact/decomposition invalidate; tags/comments/links/assignee do not.
- Integrity model (forgery/replay/staleness) inherited from the close-gate signature.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A review result is HMAC-signed and verifiable via the existing verify_signature path.
- [ ] The signature binds to the latest material-event commit; a material edit (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition) invalidates it; a non-material edit (tag/comment/link/assignee) does not — proven by tests for both.
- [ ] Coverage (sound checks run vs skipped) is recorded on the signed manifest.
data.title: Attestation: signing + material-commit binding + invalidation
